steps.title: Find the subject and verb needed
steps.content: The subject is "The Kolyma River" — singular. The relative clause "which is 1,317 miles long" is parenthetical and does not affect agreement. The sentence describes a permanent geographic fact, requiring simple present tense.

steps.title: Pick the verb that agrees
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a singular subject takes a singular verb; simple present describes ongoing geographic facts.
  • Choice A — "flows": singular simple present; correctly agrees with the singular "The Kolyma River." ✓
  • Choice B — "are flowing": plural present progressive; wrong in number for the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — "flow": plural present; does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "have flowed": plural present perfect; wrong in both number and tense for a permanent geographic fact. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
